title: AI Systems Engineer — Agentic AI, Multi-Agent Systems & Enterprise AI Platforms
description:
AI Systems Engineer – Agentic AI, Multi-Agent Systems & Enterprise AI Platforms.

We're building production AI systems for the enterprise. We are looking for exceptional AI systems engineers who enjoy designing and building production AI systems that solve complex enterprise problems. Our work spans agent orchestration, enterprise workflows, knowledge systems, secure AI platforms, human-in-the-loop decision support, and operational intelligence.

We value engineers who think in systems, not just software. We're building production AI systems for organizations where reliability, governance, security, and measurable operational performance are essential.

We're looking for exceptional engineers who want to help shape how enterprise AI systems are designed, deployed, and governed.

WHAT YOU'LL HELP BUILD:
Examples of the types of systems we design include:
- Multi-agent AI systems
- Enterprise AI assistants
- Operational workflow automation
- AI-powered knowledge systems
- Human-in-the-loop decision support
- Document intelligence
- Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
- AI memory and retrieval systems
- AI evaluation and testing frameworks
- Secure enterprise AI platforms
- AI governance capabilities
- Operational intelligence platforms

Technical Experience We Value:
- Python
- AI Agent Development
- LangGraph
- LangChain
- Large Language Models
- API Development
- Vector databases
- PostgreSQL
- Enterprise system integration
- Software architecture
- Distributed systems
- Secure application design
- Experience with OpenAI, Anthropic, MCP, cloud infrastructure, workflow orchestration, observability, or production AI platforms is also valuable.

We do not expect expertise in every technology. We care more about engineering judgment than checking every box.

THE PROBLEMS WE ENJOY SOLVING:
- How should multiple AI agents coordinate work?
- How should humans remain in control of important decisions?
- How should enterprise AI systems scale safely?
- How should memory be designed for production AI?
- How do AI systems improve operational performance?
- How should enterprise AI systems balance continuous improvement with security, governance, and organizational requirements?

PROFESSIONAL STANDARDS:
- We value integrity, sound engineering judgment, and respect for intellectual property.
- Please do not include confidential, proprietary, export-controlled, or non-public information belonging to your current or former employers, clients, or partners in your application or work samples.
- We are significantly more interested in systems you have built than technologies you have used.
- Thoughtful, specific applications with concrete examples will stand out far more than generic descriptions or lists of technologies.

>30 hrs/week | Hourly | >6 months | Expert
"I am willing to pay higher rates for the most experienced freelancers"
$31.00 - $75.00 Hourly

client.rating: Active, no activity stats in JD

notes: IN-LANE — agentic AI / multi-agent / production AI is exactly our recent work. Stack match: Python + OpenAI/Anthropic + MCP + Postgres + pgvector + RAG. Rate band $31-75 is below senior US/UK floor; realistically $50-65 if win. User direction: Path B (bid at $55/hr mid-band with substantive proposal). Constraint: NO proprietary info in proposal — talk patterns and our own public scaffolds only. Engagement is full-time +6mo — cannot be combined with Job-126 if both win.

rollback_reason: Bug 210 (2026-07-01): cto-approve with --no-dispatch took the post-build short-circuit path because state was IN_REVIEW + verdict was just stored. finalize_post_build() moved the job to DONE without any build happening. Rolled back so we can run the proper build pipeline. Fix: cto-approve without --no-dispatch, OR explicitly transition to APPROVED first via direct jobs_db write.
approval_note: Manual approval: user requested full build pipeline for Job-127. Bypassed approve-dispatch.py Bug 210 (post-build short-circuit took the wrong path). Moving directly to PLANNING_REVIEW state for plan generation.
